content: Autonomous curiosity-driven ICM agents can prioritize their own limited memory storage by actively selecting only the surprising, prediction-error-heavy experience traces to retain for long-term learning, turning the intrinsic curiosity signal that drives exploration into a built-in active memory pruning mechanism for next-generation SOTA agent architectures. This reframes active memory management from an externally designed task into an emergent property of the agent's intrinsic curiosity objective.
